The Road of the Memory Keeper

The core experience is built around a shared city map. Every report becomes a potential task, every task has an estimated reward value, and every completed cleanup can be verified through before-and-after evidence.
The demo video should show one complete story in 45-90 seconds: report a polluted place, turn it into a task, complete the cleanup, verify the result, and receive a reward.
The first version should focus on proving that users understand the loop and are willing to report or complete cleanup tasks.
The hardest challenge was managing dependencies across more than 20 teams while balancing launch deadlines against the scope of functionality being delivered.
The main challenge was evolving a financial product with many integrations and contractors while simultaneously finding opportunities for substantial operational cost reduction.
The product loop connects real-world action with immediate digital feedback.
The reward system can combine non-financial motivation with sponsor-funded payments.
The system creates a cleanup task with a visible reward value.
The system established a consistent approach to storing, processing, and using data within the Professionalitet program.
The system linked materials to educational standards and enabled reuse across disciplines and programs.
The system should be designed carefully to prevent fraud: duplicate reports, staged photos, low-quality cleanups, and reward farming need AI checks, moderation, and reputation-based limits.
The user receives points, badges, reputation, or payment.
The user uploads an after photo from the same location.
There is little immediate motivation for individuals to take action beyond personal responsibility.

When important moments happen in my life, I want to preserve them in a form that evokes emotion and helps me relive those moments.
